message:
fix(io): prevent CPU busy-loops with bounded polling steps and timer-backed fallback rewake

Several IO code paths used cx.waker().wake_by_ref() as a fallback when no
reactor registration was available, causing 100% CPU spin loops under
WouldBlock conditions. Replace all such call sites with the timer-backed
fallback_rewake() from tcp::stream, which schedules a delayed re-poll via
the timer driver instead of immediate re-wake.

fallback_rewake() visibility changed from private to pub(crate) to allow
reuse across TCP split, UDP, Unix datagram, Unix listener, and Unix stream
modules.

Additionally, Lines, ReadLine, and StreamToAsyncRead poll loops now enforce
a 32-step budget per poll invocation. When the budget is exhausted, the
waker is re-scheduled and Poll::Pending is returned, preventing any single
stream from starving the executor when a fast producer fills the buffer
faster than the consumer drains it.

Affected modules:
- src/io/lines.rs: 32-step poll budget
- src/io/read_line.rs: 32-step poll budget
- src/io/stream_adapters.rs: 32-step poll budget with partial-read awareness
- src/net/tcp/split.rs: fallback_rewake for read, write, flush, and
  reactor registration paths
- src/net/udp.rs: fallback_rewake for all WouldBlock/no-driver paths
- src/net/unix/datagram.rs: fallback_rewake for registration path
- src/net/unix/listener.rs: fallback_rewake for registration path
- src/net/unix/split.rs: fallback_rewake for read/write paths
- src/net/unix/stream.rs: fallback_rewake for registration path

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
